name: Infirst
description_short: InFirst Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering checking, savings, certificates, and share-secured loans with over 90 years of operation.
description_long:
InFirst Federal Credit Union has operated for over 90 years as a member-owned financial institution dedicated to helping members achieve financial success. As a federally-chartered credit union, it operates under NCUA insurance rather than FDIC coverage, meaning deposits are protected by the National Credit Union Administration. The organization emphasizes its commitment to personalized member service and community involvement based on testimonials featured on their website.

The credit union offers a full range of consumer banking services including FREE checking accounts (Ultimate Checking and My Money+ Checking), Prime Savings Accounts, and various certificate products. They provide share-secured loans where savings accounts serve as collateral, bill pay services, mobile banking through iOS and Android apps, and shared branching access. Current promotional offerings include youth-focused accounts with deposit matches, high-yield certificates (3.69% APY for 18 months on Youth Month specials), and referral rewards programs. Their routing number is 254075438.

InFirst distinguishes itself through emphasis on member ownership and dividends rather than traditional banking fees. The website highlights personalized service quality, with customer testimonials praising specific staff members by name and comparing favorably against larger national banks like Wells Fargo, Truist, and Carter Bank. Youth-focused promotions, including Nintendo Switch 2 giveaways and deposit matching programs, suggest targeting of younger account holders. The organization actively promotes financial education and community involvement programs.

As a credit union, InFirst requires membership eligibility and minimum account opening ($5 Prime Savings requirement). The website does not specify lending eligibility criteria, maximum loan amounts, or geographic membership restrictions. While the organization emphasizes low-interest share-secured loans, no competitive rate comparisons are provided. The emphasis on local banking and member relationships is genuine, though the website lacks detailed information about underwriting standards, approval rates, or loan terms that would allow objective comparison to other credit unions.
services: My Money+ Checking with up to 1.00% APY
Prime Savings Account with dividend earnings
Youth Savings Accounts with deposit matching
Spring Fever and Youth Month Certificate specials
Free Bill Pay with checking accounts
pros: FREE checking accounts (Ultimate Checking and My Money+ Checking) with no monthly fees
High-yield youth certificates at 3.69% APY for 18 months
My Money+ Checking earns up to 1.00% APY on checking balance
Share-secured loans with low interest rates backed by savings accounts
Youth savings account deposit matching program (up to $25 match)
Mobile banking app available on iOS and Android
Shared branching network access for members
Personalized member service with named staff recognition in testimonials
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly defined on website—unclear who qualifies
No specific loan terms, rates, or maximum loan amounts disclosed
Geographic service area and branch locations not specified on website
Limited information on loan application process or approval standards
NCUA insurance coverage differs from FDIC (though equally protective), which may confuse some consumers comparing to banks
best_for: Young people and students seeking fee-free checking with deposit matching incentives
Members seeking low-interest share-secured loans backed by savings accounts
Consumers prioritizing personalized service relationships over large-bank convenience
Individuals wanting dividend-earning savings accounts and certificate products
diagnosis: InFirst Federal Credit Union is best for members seeking personalized, fee-free checking and savings products with strong customer service emphasis, particularly younger account holders attracted to deposit matching and high-yield certificates. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility and lending criteria are not transparently disclosed on the website, requiring direct inquiry before assuming account approval.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Infirst — Credit Union in Alexandria, VA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Infirst — NCUA-insured credit union in Alexandria, VA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
